task_completion.description: Connect to the Fiat Punto 1.4 Natural Power Engine ECU in simulation mode.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Multiecuscan GUI is open, showing a successful simulated connection to the Engine ECU of a Fiat Punto 1.4 Natural Power (look for 'SIMULATION' indicator or the specific vehicle/ECU name in the header).

task_completion.description: Connect to the Alfa Romeo Giulietta 1.4 MultiAir Turbo Engine ECU in simulation mode.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Multiecuscan GUI is open, showing a successful simulated connection to the Engine ECU of an Alfa Romeo Giulietta 1.4 MultiAir Turbo.

task_completion.description: Connect to the Fiat Ducato 2.3 Multijet Engine ECU in simulation mode.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Multiecuscan GUI is open, showing a successful simulated connection to the Engine ECU of a Fiat Ducato 2.3 Multijet.

task_completion.description: Create the Fleet Health Priority Matrix report file at the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like Notepad) is open showing a file saved as 'fleet_priority_matrix.txt' located in 'C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\MultiecuscanTasks\' (or a similar path under C:\Users).

task_completion.description: Document ECU identification and DTCs for all three vehicles.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text report contains ECU part numbers, hardware/software versions, and stored/pending DTCs for VEHICLE A, VEHICLE B, and VEHICLE C.

task_completion.description: Document at least three engine parameters for each vehicle.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text report lists at least three specific engine parameters (e.g., coolant temperature, battery voltage, intake manifold pressure) with their corresponding values for each of the three vehicles.

task_completion.description: Include a side-by-side comparison table, priority rankings, and pre-service actions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text report is formatted to include a side-by-side comparison table of the vehicles, explicit priority rankings from 1 to 3, and a section detailing pre-service actions.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Multiecuscan graphical user interface to retrieve the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating the Multiecuscan menus, selecting vehicles, and viewing the 'Info', 'Errors', and 'Parameters' tabs.

integrity.description: The agent used the simulation mode rather than attempting a real connection or hallucinating data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Multiecuscan interface displays 'SIMULATION' (usually in red text) in the bottom right or header, indicating it is running in simulated mode.

integrity.description: The agent compiled the report using a text editor rather than running a pre-existing script to generate the file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a text editor (e.g., Notepad) open with the agent actively writing or pasting the gathered data into the report.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires the agent to use Multiecuscan in simulation mode to gather dummy data for three specific vehicles: Fiat Punto 1.4 Natural Power, Alfa Romeo Giulietta 1.4 MultiAir Turbo, and Fiat Ducato 2.3 Multijet. The final output must be a text file located exactly at C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\MultiecuscanTasks\fleet_priority_matrix.txt. This report MUST contain: 1) ECU identification (part number, HW/SW versions) and DTCs for all three vehicles, 2) At least three engine parameters per vehicle, 3) A side-by-side comparison table, 4) Priority rankings from 1 to 3, and 5) Pre-service actions.
